title: alsine viscosa var. hybrida
intro: Alsine viscosa var. hybrida, also known as sticky wintercress, is a plant species within the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its sticky leaves and stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of sticky wintercress include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, sticky wintercress is often used in infusions or teas, as well as in salves and creams to treat skin conditions and wounds.
